title: Hockey Legend's Sweet Legacy
story: Skates and donuts? Tim Horton mastered both! This Canadian hockey star wowed fans on the ice, then created a beloved coffee shop chain. On August 25, 1977, the Hockey Hall of Fame honored Horton's amazing career. Sadly, he wasn't there to see it. But his legacy lives on in both sports history and tasty treats!
activity_question: What did Tim Horton create besides his hockey career?
activity_choices: A new type of hockey stick
follow_up_question: Have you ever been to a Tim Hortons? What's your favorite donut?

title: Wanda Panfil's Marathon Triumph
story: Feet pounding the pavement, Wanda Panfil raced to victory in 1991. On August 25, she conquered the Women's World Championship Marathon. Panfil's incredible endurance and speed led her to finish in just over two and a half hours. Her win showcased the strength and determination of female athletes on the world stage.
activity_question: What event did Wanda Panfil win in 1991?
follow_up_question: How do you think runners prepare for such long races?

title: Victory in the Solomon Islands
story: Jungle warfare raged in the Pacific. In 1943, US forces battled through dense forests and swamps on New Georgia island. Their mission? To push back Japanese troops during World War II. After weeks of fierce fighting, on August 25, American soldiers finally secured control of the island. This victory was a crucial step in the Allied campaign to reclaim the Solomon Islands.
activity_question: What island did US forces secure control of?
bonus_fact: The Solomon Islands are home to over 900 islands, many uninhabited.

title: Austria Waves Goodbye to Soviet Troops
story: Cheers erupted across Austria as the last Soviet soldiers packed up and left the country. After a decade of occupation following World War II, Austria finally regained full independence on August 25, 1955. People celebrated in the streets, waving flags and singing. It marked the end of a long chapter and the start of a new era of freedom for Austrians.
activity_question: What did people do to celebrate the Soviet troops leaving Austria?
follow_up_question: How do you think Austrians felt when they finally regained their independence?

title: Airship Adventure Gone Wrong
story: Whoosh! A giant French airship took to the skies on August 25, 1984. But this wasn't your average flight. As the airship soared, something went terribly wrong. The massive balloon-like craft suddenly flipped upside down! Imagine being a passenger hanging on for dear life as the world turned topsy-turvy. Luckily, quick-thinking rescuers saved the day.
activity_question: What happened to the French airship during its flight?
follow_up_question: Why do you think airships are less common for travel today?

title: Breaking Barriers in Japanese Politics
story: Mayumi Moriyama shattered a glass ceiling in Japanese politics. On August 25, 1989, she became Japan's first female cabinet secretary. This historic appointment marked a significant step forward for women in government. Moriyama's achievement opened doors for future generations of female leaders in Japan.
activity_question: What position did Mayumi Moriyama become the first woman to hold in Japan?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for women to have leadership roles in government?
